calendar.matters:
Petition To Confirm Property To Trust, For Imposition Of A Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Dec-14-2020 Off Calendar

Petition To Confirm Property To Trust, For Imposition Of A Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Dec-14-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Confirm Property To Trust, For Imposition Of A Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Mar-02-2020 Continued To Sep-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John Rueppel for Petitioner, Theda Fung.

Mr. Rueppel stated that he was expecting Mr. Toscano and that he has not called in. The Court inquired as to the status of the civil case. Mr. Rueppel stated trial was scheduled for February (last month), that it has been continued to August 24th, and request status to be set for late September. The Court noted that it needs service/notice to be perfected before continuing it out 6 months. Mr. Rueppel stated that everyone has had service and that he can re-serve with the 850 language. Mr. Rueppel further stated that his client was hit by a car, possibly intentionally, that the potential beneficiaries are nervous about having their addresses on the notice of hearing, and whether notice of acknowledgement and receipt can be filed.

The amended petition to confirm property to trust, etc. is continued to September 21, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on addressing notice on petition is also set for September 21, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with filing and courtesy copies to be submitted to dept 202 at least 1 week before.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

Petition To Confirm Property To Trust, For Imposition Of A Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Aug-12-2019 Continued To Mar-02-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Vincent Yu for Theda Fung. Victor Toscano appeared by CourtCall for Susana Garcia Mancia and MGM Investments 2015 LLC (Respondents).

The Court stated before it today is the Petition To Confirm Property To Trust; For Imposition Of A Constructive Trust, the opposition to that petition, and objections. The Court stated that this is a case where there is a related civil case and addressed abatement under Probate Code 854. Argument regarding Probate Code 854 is held. Mr. Yu argued that a simple suit was filed and that Probate Code 854 isn't appropriate here. Mr. Toscano argued that the the civil action was filed for the purpose of delay, that this matter should be abated, that there are two courts now to make a determination that one court should make, that trial in the civil action should go forward on February 10, 2020, and that there is a fear that the Respondents will need to fight two courts. The Court addressed Mr. Yu stating that the petition would need to be amended to name all beneficiaries. The Court stated that the civil action covers Probate Code 854, that the civil action covers abatement, and that the civil action wasn't filed for purposes of delay. The Court then continued the Petition To Confirm Property To Trust; For Imposition Of A Constructive Trust to March 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on abatement of action. Mr. Yu stated that they intended on bringing a motion to consolidate the cases. The Court stated that if that's the wish that the motion should be brought sooner rather than later.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

Petition To Confirm Property To Trust, For Imposition Of A Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Jun-17-2019 Continued To Aug-12-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Confirm Property To Trust, For Imposition Of A Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Jun-03-2019 Continued To Jun-17-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Confirm Property To Trust, For Imposition Of A Constructive Trust
Off Calendar May-06-2019 Continued To Jun-03-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Confirm Property To Trust, For Imposition Of A Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Feb-25-2019 Continued To May-06-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Rueppel for Theda Fung.
Victor Toscano appearred by telephone for Suzanne Mancia dn MGM Investments.

Mr. Rueppel stated that Mr. Toscano is requesting abatement, that he has some questions regarding the issues raised in the Examiner's notes, that he has most of the addresses of the remainder beneficiaries and asked the Court if personal service of the 850 petition is required on the beneficiaries. Mr. Rueppel also stated that it will take more time to obtain all of the information and that he will put the required language on the Judicial Counsel form. The Court questions whether it makes sense to consolidate the probate matter with the civil action. Mr. Toscano stated that he opposes any motion to consolidate and that there are about four or five other parties in the civil action. Mr. Rueppel argues that there is no need to abate, raised the issue of statutory construction, stated that Mr. Toscano feels the civil court and the probate court are two courts, that this is one Court and requested 60-days to file the notice of hearing and his motion. The Motion to Confirm Property to Trust, etc. is continued to May 6, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition To Confirm Property To Trust, For Imposition Of A Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Jan-28-2019 Continued To Feb-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Confirm Property To Trust, For Imposition Of A Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Dec-17-2018 Continued To Jan-28-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Rueppel for Theda Fung (Petitioner).
Victor Toscano appeared by telephone for Suzanna Mancia and MGM Investments (Respondents).

Mr. Toscano stated that he filed his document on Wednesday and that a courtesy copy was delivered on Friday. Mr. Rueppel stated that it looks like an opposition, that it is not verified, that the civil matter is still ongoing, that there are many other Defendant's in the civil case and suggested this case go into discovery once they get a verified opposition. Mr. Toscano stated that this action and the civil action are the same actions, but the parties are not the same, that this matter should be abated and that the cases involve the same pieces of property. Mr. Rueppel stated that the probate case is simpler, that it involves proceeds from the sale and that the civil case has four other defendants. The Parties are to brief the issue of abatement (Probate Code 854) and they are to meet and confer as whether Mr. Rueppel will be demurring, or if the opposition is going to be verified. The Amended Petition to Confirm Property to Trust, etc. is continued to January 28, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the opposition and the issue of abatement.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Anasse. Not reported.

Petition To Confirm Property To Trust, For Imposition Of A Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Dec-03-2018 Continued To Dec-17-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Rueppel for Theda Fung (Petitioner).
Victor Toscano appeared by telephone for Respondents.

Mr. Rueppel stated that he thought the courtesy copies had been dropped off, that the civil case is in discovery and that there is no trial date. Mr. Toscano stated that the civil case has been going on for two years, that discovery is about to be completed, and that it will shed some light on the civil case and this matter. Mr. Rueppel stated that the matters need to be coordinated and that there is no opposition on file to his petition. Mr. Toscano stated that he filed his opposition and that Mr. Rueppel was served by a process server. The Court Clerk informed Mr. Toscano that his opposition was rejected and that he needed to follow up on it. The Petition to Confirm Property to Trust, etc. is continued to December 17, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for review of the amended petition and the proposed order.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: F. Ruiz. Not reported.
